title: ficus botryocarpa subsp. subalbidoramea
intro: Ficus botryocarpa subsp. subalbidoramea, a plant species within the Ficus genus, is a type of strangler fig native to the tropical regions of Asia. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to provide various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating digestive issues, and supporting immune function. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a decoction or tea, or used in herbal formulations, although its efficacy and safety as a medicinal agent have not been extensively scientifically studied.
